keyPoints: A Nash equilibrium occurs when no player can improve their payoff (result) by unilaterally changing their strategy, assuming all other players keep their strategies unchanged.
In a Nash equilibrium, no player has an incentive to deviate from their strategy, as any change would lead to a worse payoff.
A Nash equilibrium is a stable state in a game where no player can improve their outcome by changing their strategy, assuming all other players play optimally.
